projects.name: BITCOIN AVES
projects.description:
"Bitcoin Aves is a grassroots Bitcoin initiative based in Akatsi, Ghana. We focus on building a sustainable Circular Economy, teaching Bitcoin in a way that connects with local culture and needs. Our goal is to drive grassroots adoption through clear, accessible learning. At the heart of it all are values like decentralization, financial freedom, and sovereignty.
Rooted in the belief that true empowerment begins with understanding, Bitcoin Aves simplifies the complex, challenges the status quo, and champions economic independence, starting from Akatsi and rippling outward."

projects.name: Bitcoin Campus Zambia
projects.description: Bitcoin Campus Zambia is a student-led initiative focused on empowering youths and students in Zambia through Bitcoin education. We aim to build a knowledgeable and financially independent generation by teaching young people how Bitcoin works, how to earn and use sats responsibly, and how to secure their funds through self-custody. Our mission is to bring financial freedom to Zambia, starting with Lusaka's youth.
projects.initiatives: Inflation Simulator - Demonstrating the effects of inflation and how Bitcoin can serve as a hedge. Bitcoin Manifesto - Promoting Bitcoin adoption and awareness across African campuses. Starter Book - A beginner-friendly Bitcoin guide designed for students and young learners. Bitcoin Mining Education - Teaching students about mining and the technology behind Bitcoin. Bitcoin Test - Interactive tools and quizzes to help learners test their Bitcoin knowledge. Regular Campus Meetups - Hosting events and discussions to onboard new users and build a strong Bitcoin youth community.
projects.challenges: Our biggest challenge is funding. Bitcoin Campus Zambia operates as a student-led and volunteer-driven initiative, and we currently rely on small donations from supporters. Since we offer all our programs, materials, and meetups completely free to students, this model is not sustainable long-term. We need consistent funding to expand our reach, develop better educational resources, and organize more campus events.

projects.name: BitcoinBabies
projects.description: We are a non-profit organization that aims to empower mothers around the world. We take a holistic approach where we address financial empowerment through Bitcoin education, infant nutrition, and most importantly, postpartum mental wellness.
projects.initiatives: focuses on empowering mothers by providing them with Bitcoin stipends, financial literacy, and entrepreneurial skills.
projects.impact: Currently, we're deeply engaged in the Bitcoin Babies program, where we're closely monitoring 12 mothers over the span of a year. We provide weekly payouts of satoshis to enhance the well-being of both the mothers and their babies. As part of this program, we educate the mothers on topics such as entrepreneurship, financial health, and Bitcoin, and we also offer courses like cooking classes. Additionally, we're actively working to onboard merchants in the mothers' communities, enabling them to spend their satoshis locally.
projects.challenges: Getting merchants onboard who are willing to accept Bitcoin payments.

projects.name: BitcoinFriendlySA
projects.description: BitcoinFriendlySA is a platform (a local directory and content hub) that helps South Africans spend and accept Bitcoin with confidence.
projects.initiatives: Creating awareness around spending and accepting Bitcoin in South Africa via the Academy. My goal is to continue creating content on this topic and become the number one resource people turn to when they want to learn how this stuff actually works.
projects.challenges: 1. Keeping the content accurate in an ecosystem that's constantly changing. A good example of this is the Editor's note I had to include here: https://www.bitcoinfriendlysa.co.za/academy/31-top-places-to-spend-your-crypto-in-south-africa-2025. 2. Educating both the spenders and merchants who are curious about accepting Bitcoin. 3. Driving real-world spending when many still see BTC as a SoV. 4. Making some money off what I do right now to keep things ticking along. 5. Deciding where to focus my attention. I need to prioritise what content is NB and can't execute every idea that pops into my head.

projects.name: MulaStack
projects.description: MulaStack is Kenya's first Bitcoin on-ramp and off-ramp solution, designed to make saving, investing and transacting in Bitcoin effortless across Africa. We address the growing demand for Bitcoin as a reliable way to diversify portfolios and preserve wealth. Our platform enables users to seamlessly convert KES into Bitcoin via Mpesa, making it easy to save, invest and withdraw Bitcoin using just a mobile phone. MulaStack bridges the gap between local currencies and Bitcoin, empowering Africans to participate in the global Bitcoin economy without barriers.

projects.name: Orphans of Uganda Children Center
projects.description: Orphans of 🇺🇬Uganda Children Center, is a none profit/religious organizations taking care of 77 orphaned children. The Orphans of Uganda Children Center cares for 77 vulnerable orphans, providing them with food, shelter, education, and emotional support. In a country with limited resources and few funding options, the center sustains itself through Bitcoin donations, which help cover essential expenses. This innovative use of cryptocurrency allows the center to receive direct support from donors worldwide, ensuring these children have a brighter future despite challenging circumstances.

projects.name: Pay With Flash
projects.description: Pay With Flash lets African merchants accept Bitcoin payments with instant settlement into local currency or stable value. Our platform removes the complexity of crypto payments—no price risk, no custody headaches, no technical setup. Whether online or in-store, Pay With Flash makes Bitcoin a fast, modern, and practical payment option for everyday commerce.
projects.initiatives: Pay With Flash is building the payment infrastructure that turns Bitcoin into a practical, everyday payment method for African commerce. We enable merchants across retail, hospitality, e-commerce, and services to accept Bitcoin instantly with automatic settlement into local currency or stable value, removing volatility and operational risk. Through seamless APIs, hosted checkout, and in-store QR/POS tools, Pay With Flash makes Bitcoin acceptance as easy as mobile money or card payments—unlocking access to global crypto-native customers for local businesses.

projects.name: Satscode
projects.description: SatsCode is a development initiative building practical Bitcoin and decentralized tools for African communities. Our focus is creating lightweight, accessible bots and micro-apps that make Bitcoin education, payments, data, music, and user interaction easier on platforms people already use Telegram, Discord, X, and Nostr.
projects.initiatives: SatsCode is building a suite of lightweight Bitcoin developer tools, bots, and micro-apps designed for African users. Our work includes Bitcoin education bots, LNbits-based payment bots, mining simulators, blockchain exploration tools, and Nostr-powered systems — including FreeWave, a decentralized music protocol that plays signed Nostr commands. Our focus is enabling developers, communities, and educators to easily onboard, teach, transact, and experiment with Bitcoin using simple, mobile-friendly tools.
projects.challenges: SatsCode faces challenges such as limited funding for development, unreliable internet in some regions, difficulty onboarding users who have low technical literacy, and limited access to hardware for testing Bitcoin and Nostr tools. Collaboration across different African countries can also be tough due to distance and inconsistent infrastructure.

projects.name: Tapnob
projects.description: Tapnob is a Bitcoin-powered payment platform designed to simplify everyday financial transactions across Africa. We bridge the gap between Bitcoin and local banking systems by enabling instant bank transfers, fast on- and off-ramps, and seamless payments without the complexity of traditional exchanges.

projects.name: thecore21m
projects.description: EcoBitz is dedicated to achieve zero waste in glass, educate merchants who sale drinks and beverages about recycling their glass, then pay them in sats for their recycling efforts. We then resale the glass bottles after sorting and cleaning them for repackaging, making upcycled products like drinking glasses, ash trays and vaces. We are Bitcoin a Bitcoin circular economy and reaching my area recyclers to teach them about Bitcoin and how we can use it in our daily lives.

projects.name: West Data Group
projects.description: West Data Group (WDG) is a Hong Kong-based company under United International Engineering, specializing in the investment and construction of data centers. With a focus on sectors such as telecommunications, cryptocurrency mining, and AI training, WDG has developed strong partnerships with global leaders like HUAWEI, ZTE, and Canaan Inc., as well as key organizations in Ethiopia, including Ethio Telecom and Ethiopia Electric Power. In 2023, WDG expanded into Ethiopia, establishing Wes Data Center Service PLC and launching a significant 30MW data center project, positioning itself as a leader in data processing and digitalization efforts.

projects.name: What's Haute With Halima
projects.description: "What's Haute With Halima" is a dynamic Bitcoin and lifestyle podcast that breaks down the future of money in a simple, engaging, and culturally relevant way. Hosted by Halima, the show blends clear Bitcoin education, real-life storytelling, and everyday lifestyle conversations to help listeners understand how Bitcoin fits into their lives, whether they're beginners or already curious about the Bitcoin space.
projects.initiatives: My current project is "What's Haute With Halima," a Bitcoin-focused podcast where I invite Bitcoin founders, builders, and innovators to share their stories. The goal is to inspire, educate, and make Bitcoin more relatable by highlighting real journeys, challenges, and successes within the ecosystem. Through these conversations — combined with my own storytelling and lifestyle-based explanations — I simplify Bitcoin for everyday people and help them see how it connects to their lives.
projects.challenges: One of my biggest challenges is access to resources and mentorship within the global Bitcoin ecosystem, especially as I work to grow a platform that educates and inspires people at scale. I often have to navigate technical topics on my own while ensuring the content remains accurate, relatable, and accessible. Another challenge is reaching a wider audience beyond my immediate community. Although there is strong interest in Bitcoin, many people still lack foundational understanding, so I constantly need to simplify complex ideas without losing depth. I also face the challenge of balancing multiple initiatives — hosting the podcast, producing educational series, contributing to Bitcoin translations, and managing my creative businesses — all while maintaining consistency and high-quality output.

projects.name: Women Bitcoin Club

projects.name: Women of Satoshi Cooperative
